jokes.text: Do you have, like, a little throat or something?
jokes.setup: Charlie losing drinking races repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Absurd anatomical explanation for slow drinking
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific insult with absurd medical reasoning. Economy is strong.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Try this on for size. Your mother's dead.
jokes.setup: Dennis complaining about Frank's dramatic entrance
jokes.punchline: Frank drops fake death bomb as response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection and escalation. Frank's casual cruelty delivered perfectly. High shock value.

jokes.text: No, she's not dead. We're gettin' divorced though.
jokes.setup: Shock of mother being dead
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation it was manipulation
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up to the death fake-out. Shows Frank's manipulative nature.

jokes.text: You drop the bomb, then you soften the blow. You never tried this?
jokes.setup: Kids horrified by fake death announcement
jokes.punchline: Frank treats psychological manipulation as business advice
jokes.explanation: Frank's twisted business logic applied to family trauma. Perfect character work.

jokes.text: I hate the kind of person that your whore mother turned me into.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his life change
jokes.punchline: Blaming his ex-wife for his own awfulness
jokes.explanation: Frank's total lack of self-awareness. Classic deflection of responsibility.

jokes.text: Mom? She's on vacation banging one of the boys she hangs out with.
jokes.setup: Kids asking about their mother
jokes.punchline: Frank's crude description of her affair
jokes.explanation: Frank's inappropriate oversharing. Uncomfortable but fits character.

jokes.text: Well, that was awkward. / Yes, it was. / Sort of a private conversation.
jokes.setup: Witnessing intense family drama
jokes.punchline: Understated reaction to incredibly inappropriate scene
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction to chaos. The understatement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I've been manically depressed for the past couple of years. I just feel like I need a serious change, or I'm gonna kill myself.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about going to strip club
jokes.punchline: Frank casually mentions severe depression and suicidal ideation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly. Typical Sunny approach to serious topics.

jokes.text: So when you say you're giving your money away to poor people... do you mean, like, 'Mexico' poor, or can it be, like, 'me and Charlie' poor?
jokes.setup: Frank announcing he's giving money to charity
jokes.punchline: Mac trying to redefine poverty to include himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Mac's self-serving logic. Great misdirection and quotable.

jokes.text: Uh, my best friend ran me over with his car.
jokes.setup: Stripper asking what happened to Charlie
jokes.punchline: Charlie's matter-of-fact description of being hit by Dennis
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of absurd situation. Economy and character integration excellent.

jokes.text: That's the spirit that beat the Japanese!
jokes.setup: Frank reluctantly accepting strip club attention
jokes.punchline: Mac connecting WWII victory to strip club enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Absurd patriotic connection. Classic Mac's misplaced enthusiasm and nationalism.

jokes.text: I'm a man and I'm strong. I can carry heavy things. You're a woman. You're weak, and you can't.
jokes.setup: Dee questioning Dennis's claim to first pick of items
jokes.punchline: Dennis's caveman logic about gender roles
jokes.explanation: Dennis's sexist oversimplification. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Whatever your feeble little arms can carry out of here, you can keep. And the rest is mine.
jokes.setup: Dennis initially suggesting cooperation
jokes.punchline: Immediately turning it into a rigged competition
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's fake reasonableness before competition.

jokes.text: Whatever your feeble little arms can carry out of here, you can keep. And the rest is mine.
jokes.setup: Dee suggesting they work together instead of competing
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately turns it into a competition anyway
jokes.explanation: Dennis's competitive narcissism on full display - can't resist making everything about winning

jokes.text: Go, go, go! Go, horsey!
jokes.setup: Brother and sister racing to steal their father's possessions
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a horse race while committing theft
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of siblings behaving like children during theft - good cringe factor

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah. Check under my bed or under one of my pillows.
jokes.setup: Friends asking if Charlie has booze
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually revealing he keeps alcohol in bed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - casual about obviously problematic behavior.

jokes.text: Don't you roll away when I'm talking to you.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis fighting while Charlie is in wheelchair
jokes.punchline: Mac treating Charlie's wheelchair movement as disrespectful
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy reference. Mac's obliviousness to disability context.

jokes.text: Here. Hey, Charlie, here's five dollars. Go down to Wawa, pick me up a couple of sodas. I got a feeling I'll be pretty thirsty after this.
jokes.setup: Frank with strippers in Charlie's apartment
jokes.punchline: Frank treating Charlie like an errand boy before sex
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual dominance and inappropriate oversharing. Good cringe factor.

jokes.text: Oh, that is a shame. It slipped.
jokes.setup: Dennis 'accidentally' breaking Dee's lamp
jokes.punchline: Obviously fake apology for deliberate destruction
jokes.explanation: Dennis's petty vindictiveness delivered with fake innocence. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: People give you free shit, and women treat you like a puppy they found out on the street.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining benefits of being in wheelchair
jokes.punchline: Comparing pity to being a stray animal
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with specific, visual comparison. Charlie's twisted perspective.

jokes.text: Look at Sweet Dee sitting on her cloud of judgment, handing down life lessons to all the sinners.
jokes.setup: Dee criticizing their plan to fake disabilities
jokes.punchline: Dennis's elaborate biblical imagery for Dee being judgmental
jokes.explanation: Dennis's pretentious vocabulary applied to sibling rivalry. Good character work and quotable.

jokes.text: I wanna be pathetic and desperate and ugly and hopeless. / I am not ugly.
jokes.setup: Frank listing Charlie's supposed qualities
jokes.punchline: Charlie only objecting to being called ugly
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. Charlie's priorities and self-image hilariously revealed.

jokes.text: You made me sleep out in the hall. You ignored my cries for help in the middle of the night. I'll pay for your rent for six months. Twelve. Six. Ten. Six. Nine. Six. Ten. Six. Twelve. Four. Six. Deal.
jokes.setup: Charlie listing grievances against Frank
jokes.punchline: Immediately forgetting complaints when money is offered, then bizarre negotiation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - principles instantly abandoned for money, terrible at negotiation

jokes.text: I had polio. Okay? That's why I'm rocking the F.D.R. Look with the blanket and everything.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his fake disability backstory
jokes.punchline: Connecting his disguise to FDR's disability presentation
jokes.explanation: Dennis's elaborate planning and historical reference. Good absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Are you sure you're okay? It's really important that I do this myself.
jokes.setup: Dennis struggling to perform simple task
jokes.punchline: Insisting on independence while obviously struggling
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing the effort required to maintain the deception

jokes.text: I got it! All by myself, I did it.
jokes.setup: Dennis pretending to struggle with basic tasks
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top celebration of opening a door
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of fake struggle. Cringe humor but somewhat predictable execution.

jokes.text: I have polio. Oh. And I have polio... He has polio too. Mm-hmm.
jokes.setup: Dennis and Mac caught faking disabilities by Dee
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to maintain lie in face of obvious skepticism
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment of being caught - good cringe comedy execution

jokes.text: I have polio. / He has polio too.
jokes.setup: Getting caught by Dee in their disability scam
jokes.punchline: Both desperately sticking to obviously fake story
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Their panic and inability to improvise well-executed.

jokes.text: Athletes. / Yeah, that's true. / But after that. / Rock stars. / Yeah, also true. But after that, Frank. / Actors.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking who gets more respect than war heroes
jokes.punchline: Frank systematically undermining Dennis's premise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating destruction of Dennis's logic. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: Viet-goddamn-nam's what happened! Go get me a beer, bitch!
jokes.setup: Stripper asking about Dennis's 'war experience'
jokes.punchline: Dennis's over-the-top, offensive fake veteran persona
jokes.explanation: Dennis's terrible attempt at method acting. Appropriately offensive for character.

jokes.text: He's my poor little crippled boy.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining Dennis's behavior to stripper
jokes.punchline: Frank's condescending, inappropriate description
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual dehumanization. Good cringe factor and character consistency.

jokes.text: I bet you a million dollars if I had talked to a girl about my withered legs, then... / A million dollars?
jokes.setup: Dennis and Mac arguing about who ruined their scam
jokes.punchline: Mac fixating on the obviously fake bet amount
jokes.explanation: Mac's literal-mindedness about obviously hyperbolic bet. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Those two guys are pretending to be disabled and kicking each other's asses.
jokes.setup: Dennis and Mac fighting in wheelchairs
jokes.punchline: Third party's matter-of-fact description of absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect outside perspective on the absurdity. Great economy and irony.

jokes.text: Yeah, with this! I mean, the whole thing is... This is good as gold.
jokes.setup: Mac questioning Charlie's fake wheelchair setup
jokes.punchline: Charlie defending his obviously fake disability rig
jokes.explanation: Charlie's delusional confidence in his terrible fake setup

jokes.text: Frank keeps coming in. He swoops in, he takes my chicks. He doesn't care about me. What kind of a guy does that?
jokes.setup: Charlie complaining about Frank's behavior
jokes.punchline: Charlie's complete lack of self-awareness about his own terrible behavior
jokes.explanation: Charlie's victimhood complex while being equally awful. Good character irony.

jokes.text: Like I'm a piece of crap. Like I belong on the street. Like I'm some kind of piece of dirt.
jokes.setup: Charlie complaining about Frank's treatment
jokes.punchline: Escalating self-pity with increasingly dramatic comparisons
jokes.explanation: Charlie's dramatic self-pity escalating through different metaphors for worthlessness

jokes.text: War hero coming through. How you doing? War hero. So... kind of a big deal.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to pick up women with veteran status
jokes.punchline: Mac's desperate self-promotion of fake military service
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe comedy through Mac's desperate attention-seeking

jokes.text: I rigged it up. It's like... I'm really using my hands, but they don't know it.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining his makeshift wheelchair operation
jokes.punchline: Charlie's proud description of his crude deception
jokes.explanation: Charlie's engineering 'solution' and pride in deception. Good visual comedy.

jokes.text: The only thing that attracts the attention of strippers more than pity is money. And I brought a shitload of that.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining why he's successful with strippers
jokes.punchline: Frank's crude but accurate economic analysis
jokes.explanation: Frank's business logic applied to strip clubs. Sharp observation delivered bluntly.

jokes.text: If I have to listen to you talk about this stupid... goddamned stuffed elephant anymore... I'm going to punch you in the head.
jokes.setup: Dee talking about her stolen stuffed animal
jokes.punchline: Dennis's violent threat over trivial complaint
jokes.explanation: Dennis's disproportionate anger. Character-appropriate but fairly standard escalation.

jokes.text: He was like a companion. / Strip club!
jokes.setup: Dee explaining emotional attachment to stuffed animal
jokes.punchline: Frank's complete disregard and single-minded focus
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and character work. Frank's obsessive focus cutting through sentiment.

jokes.text: Mr. Tibbs was not just some stupid stuffed element... elephant, okay? He was like a companion.
jokes.setup: Dennis dismissing her stuffed elephant
jokes.punchline: Dee's emotional defense of childhood toy, with word fumble
jokes.explanation: Dee's emotional attachment to broken toy from earlier, with nervous speech pattern

jokes.text: Are you people all pretending to be handicapped? / Mind your own business.
jokes.setup: Outside observer witnessing their obvious scam
jokes.punchline: Mac's defensive response to accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with outside perspective. Mac's weak defense of obvious lie.

jokes.text: Don't youse ever stop fighting and just shut the hell up? Now, who wants ice cream?
jokes.setup: Everyone arguing in the hospital
jokes.punchline: Frank's paternal tone followed by treating them like children
jokes.explanation: Frank's attempt at fatherhood through treating adults like kids. Good tonal shift.

jokes.text: What a bunch of assholes.
jokes.setup: Witnessing the gang's behavior throughout episode
jokes.punchline: Perfect summation of characters from outside perspective
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode capper. Meta-commentary on the characters and show premise.
